questions.q: Who were the peasants according to the British colonial records?
What was the main grievance of the peasants under the Permanent Settlement?
questions.answer: They were burdened with heavy land revenue and could not afford it, leading to poverty and debt.

questions.q: What was the policy of the British government towards the ryots under the Permanent Settlement?
questions.answer: The British government maintained the rights of the ryots over the land they tilled, but the landlords were responsible for collecting land revenue from them.

questions.q: Who was the collector of the land revenue in the provinces under the British colonial rule?
What were the main features of the Permanent Settlement?
questions.answer: It was based on a fixed rate of land revenue, and the Zamindar was responsible for collecting it from the ryots.

questions.q: What was the effect of the Permanent Settlement on the ryots?
questions.answer: The ryots were burdened with heavy land revenue, leading to poverty, debt, and displacement.

questions.q: Why did the British government introduce the Permanent Settlement?
questions.answer: To regularize the land revenue collection and to reduce the burden on the state.

questions.q: What was the main difference between the Permanent Settlement and the Mahtaab Rai system?
questions.answer: The Permanent Settlement was based on a fixed rate of land revenue, whereas the Mahtaab Rai system was based on a variable rate.

questions.q: Who were the main beneficiaries of the Permanent Settlement?
What was the main grievance of the peasants against the Zamindars?
questions.answer: They were burdened with heavy land revenue and could not afford it, leading to poverty and debt.
